name: OmniSpectral EvidenceGate Reporting Disruption
old_workflow: Unstructured spectral analysis reports where peak/AUC claims, uncertainty, evidence status, and missing validation are mixed in prose.
new_workflow: Local-first spectral reporting packet with AUC/peak structure, uncertainty placeholders, DCT++ claim ledger, EvidenceGate status, and reviewer-ready missing-evidence checklist.
obsolete_assumption: A scientific report can be useful without explicitly separating measurement, claim, missing evidence, and promotion status.
bridge_object: DCT++ spectral evidence packet linking CSV-like inputs, extracted features, claims, uncertainty, and status ceiling.
advantage_metric: Time to identify unsupported claims; number of claims routed to correct gates; reviewer clarity score; reproducibility checklist completion.
minimum_demo: Run the starter kit on a sample Raman-like CSV and emit a DCT++ report plus claim ledger without asserting physical validation.
reuse_kernel: Reusable DCT++ spectral-report template and claim-routing structure.
feedback_plan: Collect 5 reviewer responses using Feedback Intake Form and convert objections into patches/tests/blocked claims.
transaction_or_impact_path: Private beta R3 -> R4 candidate only after receipt/sponsor log and delivery evidence.
rollback_path: If reviewers find the promise unclear or overclaimed, downgrade to educational reporting template and archive product claims.
next_least_action: Run ClaimRouter on public copy and collect one private reviewer feedback packet before any public launch.
notes: Disruption candidate targets workflow clarity and evidence routing, not scientific proof.
